title: vibrant flaky jamaican mussels bake
description: a nostalgic jamaican dish featuring mussels, sous-vide, and flaky crust that is low-carb-friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: preheat the oven to 375 degrees fahrenheit.
discard any broken mussels and scrub them clean.
in a large bowl, combine butter, garlic, onions, and scotch bonnet peppers.
add the mussels, flour, parmesan cheese, coconut milk, and salt to the bowl and stir to combine.
cover the bowl with plastic wrap and cook in a sous-vide water bath at 130 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
remove the bowl from the water bath and let it cool slightly.
sprinkle the top with cilantro and serve hot.
meanwhile, in a separate bowl, mix together the flour and butter until it resembles coarse crumbs.
add the flaky crust mixture to the bowl with the mussel mixture and stir to combine.
transfer the mixture to a baking dish and bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
serve the mussels hot, garnished with cilantro and a side of crusty bread.
cook time can be reduced to 10 minutes if using a conventional oven.
